task.objective: Add export functionality to the comparison results that allows users to download the comparison as Markdown (for PR comments) or JSON (for CI pipeline consumption). This enables sharing comparison results outside the dashboard.

context.project_type: E2E Test Dashboard - Compare Page Enhancement
context.background:
Users often want to share comparison results: 1. In GitHub PR comments to show test impact of their changes 2. In Slack/Teams for team visibility 3. In CI pipelines to make decisions based on comparison data
The current implementation displays results only in the dashboard UI. There is no way to export or share the comparison data.
The Markdown format should render nicely in GitHub, using: - GitHub Flavored Markdown tables - Collapsible details sections for long lists - Emojis for visual indicators
context.audience: - Developers wanting to add comparison to PR descriptions - CI/CD systems consuming comparison data - Teams reviewing test status in Slack/Teams
context.impact: LOW - Nice-to-have feature for power users and CI integration

existing_code.file:
export interface ComparisonResult { baseline: ComparisonExecutionInfo current: ComparisonExecutionInfo summary: ComparisonSummary tests: TestComparisonItem[] performanceSummary?: { regressions: number improvements: number stable: number threshold_pct: number } }
export interface ComparisonExecutionInfo { id: number branch: string commitSha: string startedAt: string totalTests: number passed: number failed: number }

export interface ComparisonSummary { baselinePassRate: number currentPassRate: number passRateDelta: number baselineTotalTests: number currentTotalTests: number testCountDelta: number baselineAvgDurationMs: number currentAvgDurationMs: number durationDeltaMs: number durationDeltaPercent: number newFailures: number fixed: number newTests: number removedTests: number unchanged: number }

functional.requirement: Add "Export" dropdown button in comparison results
Support JSON format export (for CI pipelines)
Include all summary statistics in exports
Include list of changed tests (new failures, fixed, etc.)
Add "Copy to Clipboard" functionality with user feedback
Generate collapsible details sections in Markdown

non_functional.requirement: Markdown should render nicely in GitHub comments
JSON should be valid and parseable
Show clear feedback for copy operations

solution.approach: 1. Create utility functions to convert ComparisonResult to Markdown and JSON 2. Create ExportComparisonButton component with dropdown menu 3. Use browser download API with proper cleanup 4. Add clipboard API with fallback and error handling 5. Show toast/feedback for copy success/failure

modification.description: Add export button to comparison header

solution.modification:
Add import at top: import { ExportComparisonButton } from "@/components/dashboard/export-comparison-button" In the execution info header section, add the export button:
Comparison Details
{/* ... rest of header ... */}

steps.step: 5. Open file and verify valid Markdown structure
6. Paste into GitHub PR comment and verify it renders nicely
7. Verify collapsible sections work in GitHub
4. Open file and verify it's valid JSON (try JSON.parse)
4. Paste into text editor and verify content
1. Open page in incognito or with clipboard permissions denied
3. Verify error state shows ("Failed to copy")
4. Verify download still works as fallback

always.rule: Include timestamp in JSON export for traceability
Use collapsible sections in Markdown for long lists
Escape special characters in Markdown content
Show visual feedback for copy operations
Clean up object URLs after download

never.rule: Never include sensitive data in exports
Never generate invalid Markdown or JSON
Never leave object URLs without cleanup

output_format.structure: 1. Create `lib/export-comparison.ts` with utility functions 2. Create `components/dashboard/export-comparison-button.tsx` 3. Update `app/dashboard/compare/compare-client.tsx` to include export button
output_format.validation: After implementation: 1. Verify exports are valid and render correctly 2. Test in GitHub PR comment for Markdown visibility 3. Parse JSON with jq or similar to verify validity 4. Test clipboard in different browsers
